# YourGP@Crace — Full Site Content

> General practice in Crace, Canberra — Genuine Care, Clinical Excellence.

---

## Home

A very warm welcome to YourGP@Crace.

Our practice vision is 'Genuine Care, Clinical Excellence'.

The best medicine is provided in the context of a long-term relationship with your GP. This relationship allows your GP to grow in understanding of who you are, your medical history and what is important to you. In this setting your GP can best tailor treatment to you and your circumstances, caring for you as a whole person. You can grow in trust of your GP, in their knowledge and genuine care for you and your family. Many GPs have established relationships with patients that cover decades. Some have the privilege of caring for four generations of the same family. These are relationships your GP would like to build with you.

Your GP provides clinical excellence through:
- Staying up to date with advances in medicine
- Fully computerised records and other technologies to support comprehensive care
- Continuing practice quality improvements
- Teaching the next generation of doctors by participating in the training of medical students and new GPs

Most importantly, your GP wishes to take the time to listen and explain. Your GP aims for people to leave with questions answered and a better understanding of their own health.

### Opening Hours

**Crace**: Monday to Friday 8:00am–6:00pm, Saturday 9:00am–1:00pm

### Locations

- YourGP@Crace — 1/5 Baratta Street, Crace ACT 2911
- YourGP@Lyneham — 62 Brigalow St, Lyneham ACT 2602
- YourGP@Denman — 1B Felstead Vista, Denman Prospect ACT 2611

Phone: (02) 6109 0000

---

## Doctors

### Dr Roslyn Mackenzie
**New patients welcome**
Dr Mackenzie spent 17 years in Sub-Saharan Africa doing humanitarian work as a GP obstetrician. She also has experience working as a GP in the Northern Territory and more recently has been working in Emergency at The Canberra Hospital. She is a graduate of the University of Melbourne. Dr Mackenzie enjoys the breadth of general practice and providing holistic care for patients across all stages of life.
**Availability**: Crace — Wednesday, Thursday

### Dr Mara Matic
**New patients welcome**
Mara is a caring and thorough GP who enjoys supporting patients across all stages of life. She has a special interest in women's health, chronic disease management, paediatrics, preventative care, and metabolic health. Born and raised in Canberra, Mara completed all of her medical training locally, graduating from the ANU Medical School in 2016 and becoming a fully qualified GP in 2023.
**Availability**: Crace — Monday, Thursday

### Dr Andrew Mitchell — General Surgeon
**New patients welcome** (requires GP referral)
Dr Mitchell is a specialist general surgeon. He specialises in a range of surgical services. Dr Mitchell can perform minor surgical procedures onsite at YourGP@Crace. Operations requiring general anaesthetic will be arranged at a private hospital.
**Availability**: Crace — Thursday

### Dr Tom Wigglesworth
**New patients welcome**
Dr Wigglesworth enjoys the full scope of general practice, including chronic disease management, men's health and acute medical care. He is particularly interested in dermatology and has a Masters in Clinical Dermatology from King's College London. He graduated from the University of Bristol in 2009 and completed his GP training in 2015.
**Availability**: Crace — Monday, Tuesday, Thursday, Friday

### Dr Kristy Muir
**New patients welcome**
Dr Kristy Muir is a graduate of The University of Melbourne, 2008. She particularly loves paediatrics and sports medicine and has a Masters of Sport Medicine.
**Availability**: Crace — Tuesday, Wednesday

### Dr Sheahan Ranasinghe
**New patients welcome**
Special interests include care of the elderly, acute medical care and injury management, men's health, work cover cases, dermatology and minor skin surgery. He strongly believes in evidence-based medicine and a whole of patient approach to managing care.
**Availability**: Crace — Monday to Friday

### Dr John (Kyaw) Pyae
**New patients welcome**
John takes a special interest in men's health, skin cancer medicine and performing minor procedures. John was a well-loved GP in Boorowa, NSW. He obtained his medical degree in 2012 and has almost 10 years of experience in general practice. In 2024 he was awarded the Fellowship of the Royal Australian College of General Practitioners.
**Availability**: Crace — Monday to Friday

### Dr Tom Mills
**New patients welcome**
Dr Tom Mills graduated from the Australian National University in 2018. He is currently training as a General Practice Registrar and has a special interest in travel medicine, including administering travel vaccines and yellow fever vaccinations, as well as men's health.
**Availability**: Crace — Monday, Tuesday, Thursday, Friday

### Dr Vanessa Thorn
**New patients welcome**
Dr Vanessa Thorn relocated from QLD in 2023 where she completed her GP training in 2019. She has an interest in antenatal care and paediatrics, and has completed a Diploma in Child Health (Sydney).
**Availability**: Crace — Monday, Thursday

### Dr Joanna Lin
**New patients welcome**
Joanna enjoys all areas of general practice, with special interests in paediatrics, women's health, antenatal care, and preventative health. She has completed a Diploma of Child Health with Westmead Children's Hospital. Joanna completed her undergraduate studies at UCLA before moving to Canberra for her postgraduate medical degree at ANU.

### Dr Claire Monaghan
**Only accepting existing patients of the practice and new patients for Mirena insertions**
Clare has a particular passion for paediatrics, women's health, emergency medicine, and palliative care. She graduated from The University of New South Wales in 2016 with First Class Honours.
**Availability**: Crace — Monday, Tuesday, Thursday

### Dr Lisa Bron
**Sees existing patients of YourGP** (new patients restricted to immediate family of existing patients)
Lisa graduated from Otago University in 1993. She was awarded her General Practice Fellowship in 2003 and completed her Master of Family Medicine (Monash) in 2016. Special interests include health of children and their families, mental health, chronic disease management, palliative care and medical education. Lisa is currently involved in teaching clinical skills to medical students at ANU.

---

## Services

### Children's Health
Paediatrics is a special interest of the GPs. Kids love to visit. Dr Sam Primrose has done further studies with Diplomas in Paediatrics at Westmead Children's Hospital.

### Childhood Immunisations
GPs and nurses are supportive of childhood vaccinations. They are safe and an effective way to protect your child. Immunisation records are uploaded electronically to the Australian Immunisation Register.

### Chronic Disease Management
GPs are highly experienced at helping patients with diabetes, heart disease, asthma, COPD, mental health conditions and other chronic diseases.

### Driver's Medicals
Commercial driving medical assessments require a double length appointment. For non-commercial licences for chronic medical conditions book a standard appointment.

### Men's Health
Check-ups, erectile dysfunction, incontinence, vasectomy, mental health and STI screens.

### Mental Health
Depression, anxiety, postnatal depression, schizophrenia, grief, bipolar depression and PTSD. A mental health care plan (referral to a psychologist) requires a double appointment.

### Minor Procedures
Implanon insertion and removal, IUD insertion and removal, skin cancer removal, ingrown toenail repair, tongue tie, vasectomy. Not all procedures are done by all doctors — please call the practice.

### IUD Insertion
This involves a visit to discuss IUD suitability and then a separate appointment for insertion.

### Musculoskeletal
Diagnosis and assessment of all types of joint and muscle sprains and strains.

### Tongue Tie
Dr John Deery only. Tongue tie is a common condition of newborn children. He can provide prompt assessment and treatment of the condition, on the same day, when appropriate.

### Travel Vaccinations
Yellow fever vaccine is available at Denman and Crace. Please bring any previous immunisation records.

### Skin Checks
Multiple doctors provide skin checks. Skin checks require a long appointment. Please do not wear make-up.

### Vasectomy
Initial consult with Dr John Deery at either Lyneham or Denman. The procedure is performed at Crace only on Thursday and Friday mornings. See canberravasectomy.com.au for more information.

### Women's Health
Check-ups, pap smears, STI screens, breast check, contraceptive advice, implanon insertion and removal, IUD insertion, menopause, pregnancy planning, shared antenatal care and postnatal check-ups.

### Workers Compensation
Payment is required at the time of your appointment. Standard consultation charges apply. Your insurer will reimburse you.

### Allied Health

**4Cyte Pathology**: Located next door to the practice. Opening hours are 7:30am to 12pm and 1pm to 4pm, Monday to Friday. 9am to 1pm Saturday.

**Nursing**: Immunisations, ear syringe, wound management, health assessments, ECG, iron infusions, diabetes clinics, health assessments and chronic disease care plans. Bookings only available by calling reception.

---

## FAQ

### Making Appointments
Book online via HotDoc or call the practice. If your usual GP is not available, you are welcome to see another GP at Crace, Denman or Lyneham — they will have full access to your computer record. Book a long appointment if you think your visit will require longer than fifteen minutes. Missed appointments without notice will attract a $50 fee.

### Urgent Bookings
A proportion of appointments are reserved for urgent bookings each day. You can secure an 'on the day' appointment by booking online after midnight or by calling from 8am. When fully booked, you can request a fit-in appointment.

### Billing
Payment is required at the time of consultation. The GPs do not bulk bill. Saturday standard appointments are $146 and long appointments $240. All Workers' Compensation/Third Party cases are required to pay at the consultation time.

### Parking
There is easy and plentiful parking right outside the practice. There are two disabled car parks outside the front door.

### Languages
English. A phone interpreter can be provided if necessary — please give a few days notice. Deaf patients can use the National Relay Service or Auslan interpreters.

### Confidentiality
Patient information is kept confidential. You may request the full privacy policy from reception.

### Medical Certificates
A medical certificate is a legal document. Certificates cannot be backdated. Certificates will not be issued unless your GP is consulted.

### Results
The majority of results are notified via SMS. Results are available online to view through MyHealthRecord or MyDHR.

### Contacting Your Doctor
Your GP can be contacted during business hours. Your GP will generally not take phone calls — you may leave a message.

### Repeat Prescriptions
Your GP requests that you attend for review for a repeat prescription. This is best practice medicine.

### E-Mail
E-mail to reception is not secure. To send a message to your doctor, use the contact form on the Contact page. Documents for your doctor can be sent to noreply@yourgpcanberra.com.au.

### Reminders
Your GP values preventative health care. You will be notified via SMS of reminders when due.

### After Hours Care
- CALMS (Canberra Afterhours Locum Medical Service): 1300 422 567
- Healthdirect (24/7 nurse helpline): 1800 022 222
- Walk-in Nurse Centres: 7:30am–10pm, no appointment needed

### Accreditation
Accredited with AGPAL (Australian General Practice Accreditation Limited).

### Teaching Practice
YourGP is a teaching practice. Third year medical students from ANU sit in with GPs for six-week rotations. GPs also teach and provide supervision to GP registrars.

### Suggestions or Complaints
Feedback is welcome via the contact form. If unresolved, complaints can be directed to the Community & Healthcare Complaints Commissioner, GPO Box 321, Canberra ACT 2601.

---

## Privacy Policy

YourGP maintains a comprehensive privacy policy covering collection, use, storage, and disclosure of personal health information. The full privacy policy is available at https://www.yourgpcrace.com.au/privacy-policy/
